title: Ervum leucanthum
taxonomy: Ervum leucanthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Ervum, which is a small group of plants. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the 'Mediterranean vetch'. Its classification is further detailed as belonging to the order Fabales, which includes other legumes. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its relationship with other species in the plant kingdom.
uses: Ervum leucanthum, also known as White Lupin, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been employed to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Ervum leucanthum may also have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

preparations: Ervum leucanthum is also known as Lupin, and its herbal preparations are used for various health benefits, including as a diuretic and to treat kidney stones.
